title: Lithocarpus bacgiangensis
intro: Lithocarpus bacgiangensis, also known as Bac Giang Lithocarpus, is a plant species belonging to the family Fagaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with its health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing fever, relieving coughs, and supporting immune function. Bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus bacgiangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lithocarpus bacgiangensis is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and syrups for respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus bacgiangensis is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. This family is commonly known as the beech family or the oak family. It is further classified into the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees native to Asia. The species Lithocarpus bacgiangensis is specifically found in Vietnam, where it is considered an endangered species. The classification of Lithocarpus bacgiangensis is also known as a member of the order Fagales.
uses: Lithocarpus bacgiangensis, a plant species found in Vietnam, has been reported to possess medicinal properties. The bark of Lithocarpus bacgiangensis is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever and inflammation.
